event: treated at the Pulmonary Department of sixth Minsk city clinical hospital
breath sounds were reduced in the lower segments of the lungs
Chest CT scans demonstrated a bilateral peripheral ground-glass attenuation
Nasopharyngeal SARS-CoV-2 RT-PCR test was positive
oxygen supplementation through the simple face mask with a flow rate of 4 l/min
saturation with free breathing decreased to 90%
air in the right pleural cavity
pleural effusion in the right pleural cavity
The upper level of pleural fluid was located at the level of the third rib
transferred to the Intensive Care Unit
thoracostomy in the 6th intercostal space on the mid-axillary line
1400 ml of a yellowish opaque liquid was evacuated from the pleural cavity
lack of air in the right pleural cavity (follow-up chest radiograph)
oxygen supplementation through the simple face mask with flow rate of 8-10 l/min
Daily drainage volume was from 300 to 1000 ml of fluid
pleural effusion with gas bubbles extended along the posterior and inferior walls of the right hemithorax
The right lung was reduced in volume by half
a focal area of subpleural infiltration with a central cavity of destruction up to 6.5 mm in diameter (S6 segment)
Air layer up to 47 mm of anteroposterior thickness along the anterior chest wall (right)
Radiological sings of the left side hydropneumothorax
pleural effusion with 25 mm of anteroposterior depth (left)
Air layer up to 19 mm of anteroposterior thickness along the anterior chest wall at the level of the lingular segments of the lung
needle thoracocentesis in the second intercostal space on the midclavicular line
Air and creamy purulent mass have been aspirated
200 to 800 ml of serofibrinous hemorrhagic fluid was drawn daily through 2 drainage tubes
Acinetobacter baumannii cultured in the pleural fluid
Pseudomonas aeruginosa cultured in the pleural fluid
urine culture was positive for Klebsiella pneumonia
Chest CT showed the presence of air and pleural effusion in the right hemithorax
plasma D dimer level was 2424 ng/ml
diagnosis of pleural empyema was confirmed
transferred to the Surgical Department of the 10th Minsk city clinical hospital
right pleural space was daily irrigated with antiseptic solutions
Lung expansion was achieved by continuous vacuum aspiration technique
colistimethatum natrium (1 million units 2 times a day) started
imipenem/cilastatin (1 g 3 times a day) started
encapsulated pleural effusion located in the upper anterior area of the right hemithorax identified
Ultrasound-guided puncture of this effusion was performed
a new drainage of the pleural cavity was installed
discharged from the hospital for outpatient follow-up in satisfactory condition
Oxygen saturation (SpO2) was 97% on room air
A chest CT after chest tube removing showed the presence of a small amount of fluid in the right pleural cavity
Lab test scores came back to normal range
